title: The Growing Case for Tourist Taxes
subtitle: 旅游税：地方财政的新出路

paragraphs.en: Nicola Sturgeon's speech last Tuesday setting out the Scottish government's legislative programme for the year ahead confirmed what was already pretty clear. Scottish councils are set to be the first in the UK with the power to levy charges on visitors, with Edinburgh likely to lead the way. Tourist taxes are not new.
paragraphs.zh: 尼古拉·斯特金上周二的讲话阐述了苏格兰政府未来一年的立法计划，证实了业已明朗之事。苏格兰各议会将成为英国首批有权向游客征收费用的地方机构，爱丁堡可能率先施行。旅游税并非新鲜事物。

paragraphs.en: The Himalayan kingdom of Bhutan has a longstanding policy of charging visitors a daily fee. France's tax on overnight stays was introduced to assist thermal spa towns to develop, and around half of French local authorities use it today. But such levies are on the rise.
paragraphs.zh: 喜马拉雅的不丹王国长期实行向游客收取每日费用的政策。法国对过夜住宿征税最初是为了帮助温泉小镇发展，如今约半数法国地方当局仍在沿用。但此类征税正在增加。

paragraphs.en: Moves by Barcelona and Venice to deal with the phenomenon of over-tourism through the use of charges have recently gained prominence. Japan and Greece are among the countries to have recently introduced tourist taxes. That the UK lags behind is due to our weak, by international standards, local government, as well as the opposition to taxes and regulation of our aggressively pro-market ruling party.
paragraphs.zh: 巴塞罗那和威尼斯通过收费应对过度旅游现象的举措近来备受瞩目。日本和希腊等国最近也开征了旅游税。英国之所以落后，在于按国际标准衡量地方政府较为弱势，加之执政党极力推崇市场、反对税收与监管。

paragraphs.en: Some UK cities have lobbied without success for the power to levy a charge on visitors. Such levies are no universal remedy as the amounts raised would be tiny compared with what has been taken away by central government since 2010. Still, it is to be hoped that the Scottish government's bold move will prompt others to act.
paragraphs.zh: 英国一些城市曾游说争取向游客收费的权力，但未获成功。此类征税并非万能药，因为与中央政府自2010年以来削减的资金相比，筹集的金额微不足道。尽管如此，仍希望苏格兰政府的大胆举措能促使其他地方行动起来。

words.meaning: n. 补救办法，解决措施

paragraphs.en: There is no reason why visitors to the UK, or domestic tourists on holiday in hotspots such as Cornwall, should be exempt from taxation — particularly when vital local services including waste collection, park maintenance and arts and culture spending are under unprecedented strain. On the contrary, compelling tourists to make a financial contribution to the places they visit beyond their personal consumption should be part of a wider cultural shift.
paragraphs.zh: 没有理由让访英游客或在康沃尔等热门地区度假的国内游客免税，尤其是当垃圾收集、公园维护及文化艺术支出等重要地方服务面临空前压力之时。相反，促使游客在个人消费之外为其所访之地做出经济贡献，应成为更广泛文化转变的一部分。

paragraphs.en: Westerners with disposable incomes have often behaved as if they have a right to go wherever they choose with little regard for the consequences. Just as the environmental harm caused by aviation and other transport must come under far greater scrutiny, the social cost of tourism must also be confronted. This includes the impact of short-term lets on housing costs and quality of life for residents. Several European capitals, including Paris and Berlin, are leading a campaign for tougher regulation by the European Union. It also includes the impact of overcrowding, litter and the kinds of behaviour associated with noisy parties.
paragraphs.zh: 拥有可支配收入的西方人常常表现得好像有权想去哪就去哪，却很少顾及后果。正如航空及其他交通造成的环境危害必须受到更严格审视一样，旅游业的社会成本也必须被正视。这包括短租对居民住房成本和生活质量的影响。巴黎、柏林等几个欧洲首都正在牵头推动欧盟加强监管。它还包括过度拥挤、乱扔垃圾以及喧闹聚会相关行为带来的影响。

paragraphs.en: There is no one size fits all solution to this problem. The existence of new revenue streams for some but not all councils is complicated, and businesses are often opposed, fearing higher costs will make them uncompetitive. But those places that want them must be given the chance to make tourist taxes work.
paragraphs.zh: 这个问题没有一刀切的解决方案。部分而非全部议会拥有新收入来源的情况颇为复杂，且企业常常反对，担心成本上升会使它们失去竞争力。但那些希望实施旅游税的地方必须获得机会让其发挥作用。
